welcome.title: Welcome to Paper Trading
welcome.description: Trade Gold with $10,000 virtual funds using real market data. Your positions react to live XAUUSD price — including auto-liquidation, stop losses, and take profits — just like a real exchange.
welcome.example: This is a safe sandbox. Learn from mistakes here, not with real money.

positionMode.title: Position Mode
positionMode.description:
One-Way: you can only hold one direction (LONG or SHORT) at a time. Opening the opposite closes your current position.
Hedge: hold LONG and SHORT simultaneously to hedge risk.
positionMode.example: Start with One-Way. Hedge mode is for advanced strategies.

marginMode.title: Margin Mode
marginMode.description:
Cross: your entire balance backs every position. Lower liquidation risk, but one bad trade can drain everything.
Isolated: only the margin you assign is at risk. Max loss = your margin for that trade.
marginMode.example: Isolated is safer — you can never lose more than your position size.

direction.title: LONG or SHORT
direction.description:
LONG: you profit when Gold price goes UP. You're betting the price will rise.
SHORT: you profit when Gold price goes DOWN. You're betting on a drop.
The wrong direction + high leverage = fast liquidation.
direction.example: Not sure? Check the AI Advisor tab for signal direction before choosing.

orderType.title: Market vs Limit Order
orderType.description:
Market: fills instantly at the current Gold price. Simple and guaranteed.
Limit: you set a specific entry price. The trade only opens if Gold reaches that price.
orderType.example: Use Market to practice quickly. Use Limit to get better entries on pullbacks.

entryPrice.title: Entry Price
entryPrice.description:
This is the price you enter the trade at.
Market order: auto-filled with the live Gold price (read-only).
Limit order: type your desired entry — it must be below current price for LONG, above for SHORT.

leverage.title: Leverage — The Risk Multiplier
leverage.description:
Leverage multiplies BOTH your profits AND losses.
At 10x: a 1% Gold move = 10% gain or loss on your margin.
At 100x: a 1% move = 100% — you get liquidated instantly.
Higher leverage = liquidation price closer to entry.
leverage.example: Beginners: stay at 2-5x. Even pro traders rarely go above 20x.

size.title: Position Size (Margin)
size.description:
How much USD you put up as collateral.
Notional value = Size x Leverage. This is your actual market exposure.
The % buttons let you quickly allocate a fraction of your balance.
size.example: $10 at 10x = $100 exposure. If Gold moves 10%, you gain or lose $10 (100% of margin).

stopLoss.title: Stop Loss — Your Safety Net
stopLoss.description:
The price where your position auto-closes to limit losses.
When Gold hits your SL, the system automatically closes your trade — no action needed.
Without a SL, your only exit is liquidation or manual close.
stopLoss.example: Rule of thumb: set SL where your trade thesis is invalidated, usually 1-2% from entry.

tp1.title: Take Profit 1 — Lock In Early Gains
tp1.description:
Your first profit target. On real exchanges, this would close 40% of your position.
TP1 should be a realistic, near-term target based on support/resistance levels.
The R:R ratio shown tells you the reward relative to your risk.
tp1.example: If your SL is 2% away, a good TP1 is at least 2% profit (1:1 R:R).

tp2.title: Take Profit 2 — Secure Most Profits
tp2.description:
Second target closes another 40% of your position.
By TP2, you've locked in 80% of your profits — the remaining 20% rides for free.
Set this at a medium-term resistance/support level.

tp3.title: Take Profit 3 — The Moon Target
tp3.description:
Your final target. When Gold hits TP3, the system auto-closes your entire position at full profit.
This is your ambitious "moon bag" target.
The position auto-closes here — you don't need to watch it.
tp3.example: Aim for 3:1 R:R or higher on TP3. Let winners run!

preview.title: Order Preview — Check Before You Trade
preview.description:
Review everything before submitting:
- Liquidation Price: where you lose 100% of margin
- Max Loss: your worst case (at SL)
- Max Gain: your best case (at TP3)
- R:R Ratio: reward divided by risk (aim for 2+ )
preview.example: If R:R is below 1.5, your risk is too high relative to reward. Adjust your levels.

submit.title: Open Your Position
submit.description:
Hit this button to open your paper trade with live Gold data.
Green = LONG (you're bullish).
Red = SHORT (you're bearish).
Once open, the system monitors your trade 24/7.

positions.title: Active Positions — Live Monitoring
positions.description:
Your open trades update with the live Gold price.
Watch your unrealized P&L, TP progress bar, and liquidation distance.
The system auto-closes trades when SL, TP3, or liquidation price is hit — just like a real broker.
positions.example: Trades are checked every 5 minutes. You can also close manually anytime.

liquidation.title: Liquidation — The Biggest Risk
liquidation.description:
If Gold moves far enough against you, your position gets liquidated.
Liquidation = you lose 100% of your margin on that trade.
Higher leverage = liquidation price closer to entry.
At 100x LONG, just a ~1% drop liquidates you.
liquidation.example:
10x LONG at $100k → liquidated at ~$90k.
100x LONG at $100k → liquidated at ~$99k.

history.title: Trade History — Learn From Every Trade
history.description:
Every closed trade is recorded with:
- Win/Loss result and exact P&L
- Close reason (manual, stop_loss, take_profit, liquidated)
- Entry to exit price and trade duration
Review your history to spot patterns in your wins and losses.

portfolio.title: Portfolio — Track Your Journey
portfolio.description:
Your overall stats: balance, total P&L, and win rate.
The progress bar tracks your journey from $10 to $10,000.
Aim for consistent small wins rather than big risky bets.
portfolio.example: A 60% win rate with 2:1 R:R is highly profitable long-term.

riskManagement.title: Golden Rules of Trading
riskManagement.description:
Never risk more than 1-2% of balance per trade.
Always use a stop loss — no exceptions.
Don't increase leverage after a loss (revenge trading).
Take breaks after 3 consecutive losses.
The goal is survival first, profits second.
riskManagement.example: Most traders blow up from over-leveraging, not from bad direction calls.

finish.title: You're Ready to Trade!
finish.description:
Everything here uses real Gold price data with realistic mechanics:
- Auto-liquidation at your liquidation price
- Auto-close at stop loss and take profit
- Real-time P&L tracking

Start small, learn the patterns, and build your edge.
Good luck on your $10 to $10,000 journey!
